title: pistacia eurycarpa
intro: Pistacia eurycarpa is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean, belonging to the Anacardiaceae family and commonly known as the Long-bracted Pistachio or Long-bract Pistachio. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its fruits, leaves, and bark are used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Pistacia eurycarpa include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing cardiovascular risks and improving overall well-being. Traditionally, the plant's parts are prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, or are used in combination with other herbs in folk medicine to address digestive issues and other health concerns.
